title: Listen and Choose a Response — Library & Resources

questions.context: Asking about library hours during break
questions.stem: Is the library open during spring break? I need a place to study.

options.A: Yes, but with reduced hours. It's open from 10 AM to 6 PM.
options.B: Spring break is a week long.
options.C: I'm going home for spring break.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about library hours during a holiday, so providing the specific schedule is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: How many books can I check out at once with my student ID?

options.A: Undergraduate students can borrow up to 30 books at a time.
options.B: Books can be renewed online if no one else needs them.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about borrowing limits, so providing the specific number directly answers their question.

questions.stem: What if the library doesn't have the book I need? Can you get it from another library?

options.A: Yes, we offer interlibrary loans. It usually takes about a week.
options.B: Libraries share resources through a network.
options.C: I found my book at a used bookstore.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about alternative access options, so explaining the interlibrary loan service and timeline is helpful.

questions.stem: Are there scanners available in the library for digitizing documents?

options.A: Yes, there are scanners on the first floor near the reference desk.
options.B: Digital copies are easier to organize.
options.C: I use a scanning app on my phone.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about available equipment, so confirming availability and providing the location is helpful.

questions.stem: The research librarian was incredibly helpful with my thesis sources. I just wanted to say thanks.

options.A: That's wonderful to hear. I'll make sure the librarian gets your feedback.
options.B: Research librarians have advanced degrees in library science.
options.C: I should visit the research desk more often.

questions.explanation: The speaker is giving positive feedback about library staff, so acknowledging and promising to share the appreciation is professional.

questions.stem: Can I borrow a laptop from the library if mine is being repaired?

options.A: Yes, we have laptops available for 24-hour checkout at the circulation desk.
options.B: Laptops are essential for college students.
options.C: I bought my laptop during orientation week.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about temporary equipment access, so confirming availability and explaining the checkout process is helpful.

questions.stem: Which floor of the library is the silent study area? I need complete quiet.

options.A: The fourth floor is designated as a silent study zone. No talking allowed.
options.B: Quiet study areas are popular during exam week.
options.C: I study best with background music.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about specific study environment locations, so providing the exact floor and its rules is the most useful response.

questions.stem: Just a heads up — the book you borrowed is due tomorrow.

options.A: Thanks for the reminder! I'll return it later today.
options.B: Books can be renewed twice per semester.
options.C: I returned my books last week.

questions.explanation: The speaker is giving a friendly reminder, so expressing thanks and confirming the action you'll take is the appropriate response.
